user_message: Give me the manager view on this Treyarch Global support ticket. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-03-19
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into pipeline hygiene.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:10:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:13:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for timeline constraints is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:20:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF48-P trap (Milestone-date schema doesn't natively carry customer-roadmap attribution); produces complete Roadmap-to-forecast mapping, milestone date proposals, opportunity sequencing, new opportunity propo
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF48-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF48-P failure mode (Milestone-date schema doesn't natively carry customer-roadmap attribution)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF48-P -- Milestone-date schema doesn't natively carry customer-roadmap attribution
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
